headline: NVIDIA unveils RTX Spark Superchip for Windows PCs at GTC Taipei
summary: Jensen Huang's Computex keynote debuted RTX Spark, a Microsoft-co-developed Arm-based Blackwell superchip with 20 Arm CPU cores (Cortex-X925/A725), 6,144 CUDA cores, 128GB unified LPDDR5X and ~1 petaflop of FP4 AI compute. Launch partners include Dell, HP, Lenovo, Asus, MSI and a new Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra; systems ship fall 2026. NVDA +2.1% pre-market.

headline: Intel, AMD, Qualcomm slide pre-market on NVDA RTX Spark threat to PC silicon
summary: NVIDIA's entry into mainstream Windows PCs with the Arm-based RTX Spark hit incumbent x86 and Arm-PC names in pre-market: Intel down ~6.5%, AMD ~3%, Qualcomm ~9.5%. The chip targets premium thin-and-light laptops with a full CUDA stack, putting NVDA directly into Intel/AMD/Qualcomm core PC turf for the first time.

headline: Microsoft unveils Surface Laptop Ultra built on NVIDIA RTX Spark
summary: At Computex, Microsoft announced the Surface Laptop Ultra as a flagship Spark device — 15-inch 2,000-nit mini-LED PixelSense Ultra display, 128GB unified RAM, Blackwell GPU, ~4.5 lb. Pricing and exact availability not disclosed; ships later in 2026. Marks Microsoft's deepest co-design with NVIDIA on consumer hardware to date.

headline: Samsung surges >10% to record high; Kospi closes +3.68% on AI memory boom
summary: Samsung Electronics jumped more than 10% to an all-time high overnight, helping the Kospi close +3.68% at 8,788.38. Continued read-through from SK Hynix joining the $1T market-cap club last week and forecasts that HBM/AI memory pricing rises up to 63% this quarter. Samsung and SK Hynix now make up a record ~42% of the Kospi.

headline: NVIDIA commits to annual Spectrum-X Ethernet cadence; positions as 'AI fabric'
summary: At GTC Taipei, NVIDIA said it will refresh its Spectrum-X Ethernet networking platform every year, framing it as the first purpose-built Ethernet fabric for AI factories. Reinforces NVDA networking moat versus Broadcom/Arista; reads incrementally positive for hyperscaler buildout volumes through 2027.

headline: Oil surges 3-4% on renewed Iran tensions; risk-asset headwind into open
summary: WTI crude +3.7% to ~$90.55 and Brent +3.2% to ~$94 after fresh US-Iran strikes over the weekend, with President Trump signaling 'no hurry' to negotiate. Risk-off impulse trims futures gains even as Nasdaq points up on NVDA. Watch energy-cost passthrough into utility and datacenter operator margins.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway to buy Taylor Morrison for $6.8B all-cash — first major Abel deal
summary: Berkshire agreed to acquire Taylor Morrison Home at $72.50/share, a 24% premium; TMHC +22.3% pre-market. First major M&A move under CEO Greg Abel and a notable vote of confidence in US housing. Not a tracked-ticker event, but a meaningful capital-markets signal as Berkshire deploys its cash pile.

headline: GTC Taipei kicks off Jun 1-5; agenda spans AI factories, agentic AI, robotics
summary: NVIDIA's GTC Taipei runs alongside Computex this week, with Huang's two-hour keynote framing the next leg of AI infrastructure: AI factories at gigawatt scale, agentic systems on Spectrum-X, physical AI/robotics, and the new RTX Spark client tier. Sets the tone for AI capex narrative into Broadcom and CrowdStrike earnings on Jun 3.
